test_text.title: Arranging a Host Family for a Student

audioscript.text: Section 1, you will hear a conversation between a student looking for a host family and a housing advisor.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example which has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Um. I understand you help fix up students with host families.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Are you interested in...?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Oh. Well, please sit down, and I'll just take a few details.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Thank you.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Right, now, what name is it?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Jenny Chan.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Can you spell that please?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes, JENNY CHAN.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: And what is your present address?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: The student's name is Jenny Chan.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So Jenny Chan has been written on the form.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully, and answer questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Um. I understand you help fix up students with host families.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Are you interested in...?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, please sit down, and I'll just take a few details.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Thank you.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Right, now, what name is it?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Jenny Chan.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Can you spell that please?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes, JENNY CHAN.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: And what is your present address?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Sea View Guest House, 14 Hill Road.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: OK, and do you know the phone number there?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes, I have it here.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: uh, 2 2 3 7 6 7 6, but I'm only there after about 7 pm.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: So when would be the best time to catch you?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: I suppose between 9 and let me see, half past, before I leave for the college.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Great, and can I ask you your age?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: I've just had my 19th birthday.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: And how long would you want to stay with the host family?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: I'm planning on staying a year, but at the moment, I'm definitely here for 4 months only.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: I have to get an extension to my permit.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: You're working on it?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Mhm!
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: And what will be your occupation while you're in the UK?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Studying English?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: And what would you say your level of English is?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Ha, um, good, I think I'd like to say advanced, but my written work is below the level of my spoken.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: So, I suppose it's intermediate.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Hmm. Certainly your spoken English is advanced.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Anyway, which area do you think you would prefer?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Ummm, well, I'm studying right in the center, but I'd really like to live in the northwest.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: That shouldn't be a great problem.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: We usually have lots of families up there.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Uh. And before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: And do you have any particular requirements for diet?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Well, I'm nearly a vegetarian, not quite.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Shall I say you are?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: It's probably easier that way.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yeah, that would be best.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Anything about your actual room?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Uh. I would prefer my own facilities - 'en suite', is that right?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: And also, if it's possible a TV.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: And I'd also like the house to have a real garden, rather than just a yard.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Somewhere I could sit and be peaceful.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Is that all?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Well, I'm really serious about improving my English, so I'd prefer to be the only guest, if that's possible.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: No other guests.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes, you get more practice that way.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Anyway, obviously all this is partly dependent on how much you're willing to pay.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: What did you have in mind?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: I was thinking in terms of about 60 to 80 pounds a week, but I'd go up to 100 if it was something special.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, I don't think we'd have any problems finding something for you.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Oh, good.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: And when would you want it for?
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: I'd like to move in approximately two weeks.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Let me see, it's the 10th today, so if we go for the Monday, it's the 23rd of March.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: Yes, right, good, and if I could ask one last question.
audioscript.speaker: ADVISER

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: An Update on the Soccer Club for the Coming Season

audioscript.text: Section 2, you will hear a speech by an official at a meeting of a local football club, at the start of a new football season.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First you have some time to look at questions 11 to 17.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully, and answer questions 11 to 17.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good evening everyone, and thank you for coming to the soccer club meeting.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's good to see so many parents and children here tonight, and I know you're looking forward to a great football season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, I'd like to take a few minutes to tell you about some changes to the soccer club for the coming season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now this season, we'll be playing all our matches for both the junior and senior competitions at King's Park.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Instead of Royal Park, which was used last season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now for meetings, we're going to use the clubhouse in King's Park, and the next meeting will be held in the clubhouse on the 2nd of July.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As usual, we hope to begin the season with a picnic next Saturday at the clubhouse.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. Please try and come to the picnic, as it's always good fun.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The last week of the season, we usually have a dinner and presentation of prizes to the players.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And more information about this will be given to you later in the season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This season, we have more teams than ever.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We hope to have 10 teams instead of five in the junior competition.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And they'll play on Saturday mornings, beginning at 8:30 a.m.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Training sessions will be held in King's Park on Wednesday afternoons for the juniors and they will be wearing red shirts again this year.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In the senior competition, there will be four teams, the same as last year, and their games will be played on Saturday afternoons, starting at 2:30, no, sorry, it will be a 2:00 start, and the training session for seniors is planned for Sunday afternoons.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the speech, you have some time to look at questions 18 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 18 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now, I'd like to introduce you to the new committee for the soccer club for this season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. Firstly, let me welcome Robert Young, the new president, who will manage the meetings for the next 2 years.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Robert's son has been playing football with the club for over five years now, and many thanks to Robert for taking on the job of president.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Next we have Gina Costello.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: She's the treasurer, and she'll collect the fees from you for the season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. Please try and give Gina your fees as early as possible in the season.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As the club needs the money to buy some new equipment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Then there's David West, who's volunteered to be the club secretary, and one of the many jobs he'll have is to send out newsletters to you regularly.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: If you have any information that may be useful, please let David know, so that it can be included in these newsletters.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Also, I'd like to introduce you to Jason Dokic, who is the Head Coach.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: For all the new members here tonight, this is the third year that Jason has been with us as head coach, and we're very lucky to have such an experienced coach and former player at our club.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: He will continue to supervise the teams at training sessions and on match days.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, before we finish and have some refreshments, does anyone have any questions they'd like to ask the new committee?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items: The next meeting of the soccer club will be in the <input> in King's Park on 2 July.
At the final dinner, players receive <input>.

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

answer_key.correct_answer: collect fees | collect money | collect the fees | collect the money
send newsletter | send newsletters | send out newsletter | send the newsletter | send out newsletters | send the newsletters

test_text.title: Box Telecom's Challenges and Possible Ways Forward

audioscript.text: Section 3, you will hear a tutor and two students discussing a business case study.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Right, Jason and Karin.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Now I asked you to look at the case study for Box Telecom, as part of your exam assessment.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: It's interesting, because they're in the middle of problems at the moment, and I want you to track how they deal with them.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Um. Let's start with you, Karin.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Having read through the case study, can you just summarize what the problems were that Box Telecom had to take on board?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Um. Yeah, um, well of course what first came to their attention was that despite a new advertising campaign, they were suffering from falling sales.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: And this is something that had many causes.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: On top of that immediate problem, what had also happened over the last two years was that although they had invested in an expansion plan, they had to face up to increased competition.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: And before they had a chance to get to grips with the effects of that, they were stalled by a strike.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: And it was just when they were thinking about making a colossal investment in new machinery for their plants.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: So they were really in trouble.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: Yes, I think that's fair.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And Jason, um, now you contacted the company, didn't you?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Hmm. What did the company define as the reasons for these problems?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Well, I think they hit on the right things.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: It would be easy to say they had invested too heavily or at the wrong time, but in fact the signs were good, and what they were set back by was high interest rates.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: At the same time, their longer term problems which were affecting their market share were eventually credited to poor training, and having looked at the details in their last report, I think that's right.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So, onto the larger issues then, Karin, what do you think the company will do?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Hmm. Well, obviously they have the choice of accepting the very favorable terms that another company - KMG Plc - have given them to buy them out.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: That would mean creating a new company with a new image, or they could decide on a bolder move and offer some new shares if they wanted.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: But I think they're much more cautious than that, and expect they will start trying to find individuals who'd be prepared to back them with some of the capital they need.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: Well, you mustn't always assume that dramatic problems require dramatic solutions.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Sometimes there's a simple fix, such as changing the guy at the top.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: If they truly are cautious, then I suspect they will seek to shut down some of their shops.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: But a more ambitious approach, and one which I think would have more chance of success, would be to alter how they're running things - the management layers and the processes.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: So, in your analysis, try to think of all the options.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Jason?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, it's interesting because I've found it a really useful company to study.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: Its problems cross all types of industries, and it's lucky it's so big.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: A smaller or even medium-sized company would have gone under by now.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: Oh, well, in fact, what I want you two to do is to go away when we've finished our discussion today.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And write a report.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: We've looked in general at the telecommunications market in the UK over the last few sessions, and I want you to take Box Telecom as an example.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And suggest some ways in which they might overcome their problems, and outline the reasons why you think as you do, but try and keep it intrinsic to the company.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Rather than dragging in other examples. Is that OK, Karin?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, I think I can do that.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: Uh. Personally, I've got great hopes for it.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: I think it will recover.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: That advertising campaign they did was very strong, and they're very innovative with their products.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: They set new trends.
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: The company's got to recover, don't you think, Jason?
audioscript.speaker: KARIN

audioscript.text: Hmm, I'm not sure.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: I think it can, but it's not a foregone conclusion, unless they manage to attract the right level of investment.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: The company definitely needs a boost.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: And to attract more highly skilled workers if their recovery is to be long lasting.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: When I was talking to the marketing manager, he said to me that he thinks the company had got a great management team.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: But he would say that wouldn't he?
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: But they are suffering from having to work with outdated production machinery, and that could cost a lot to put right.
audioscript.speaker: JASON

audioscript.text: Well, personally, I think the stock market is to blame.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I think they were expecting too much of the company, and then inevitably it looked bad when it didn't perform.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: The market should have had more realistic expectations.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And I disagree with you about the advertising campaign, Karin.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: That's where they could do with some innovation - to get sales kick-started.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Anyway, let's see what you come up with...
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: What does Karin think the company will do?
options: look for private investors
accept a takeover offer
issue some new shares

items.title: How does the tutor suggest the company can recover?
options: by appointing a new managing director
by changing the way it is organised
by closing some of its retail outlets

items.title: The tutor wants Jason and Karin to produce a report which
options: offers solutions to Box Telecom's problems.
analyses the UK market.
compares different companies.

text: Which opinion does each person express about Box Telecom?
textReadable: Which opinion does each person express about Box Telecom?

test_text.title: Cardiff University Library: Resources, Services, and Training Offerings

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear a university librarian giving a talk to new students.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: OK, are you all settled?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, first of all, welcome to Cardiff University.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: I'm here to explain what we can offer you.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, as a new student at the university, you will probably need some sort of guidance to help you to use the library effectively to study and research.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Some of you have asked about a guided tour, but we find this rather muddles people.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So in this first week we run a series of talks which focus on different aspects of the library and its resources.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You'll also find that to get the most out of the library, you really do need to be computer literate.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And so all this term we run small classes, which will bring you up to speed on how to access the computer loaded information.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: OK, now let me give you an outline of what's available to you.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You'll find that the computers are increasingly used as a research tool.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Many students do most of their research on the internet, and the library computers are permanently online.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Having found what you need, you'll find you can readily save texts on your personal computer space to print off when you need.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You might think that it is the fastest way to get information, but the links can be slow.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Clearly, you can find lots on there, but much of it is useless information, as it is from highly debatable sources, so be critical.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You'll also find that the library has loaded several CD-ROMs onto the computers from specialist reference sources.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Such as the MLA.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It means we can expand what we offer you at very little extra cost, and saves us having to invest in more and more books.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The CD-ROMs contain exactly the same information as the reference books, as the two are updated together.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now most of you will need to refer to journal articles in your work, and you'll find you can also access these online, and we encourage you to do so.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Clearly some of you will find the printed version more accessible as it sits on the shelves.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But I'm afraid the intention is to phase these out eventually.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: However, you will still be able to print off a version of the text, rather than photocopying the journal pages.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So you must get used to working online.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Naturally, we do still have the full range of classic reference books, additional to the CD-ROMs for you to use.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And there are several copies of each one.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This is because some of you may prefer to borrow a book rather than sit in the library.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There is a restricted loan time on these, so that they are not missing from the shelves for too long.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Although there is a section manager for each part of the library, they are very busy, and so if you do get stuck looking for things, you should ask the relevant Cataloging Assistant.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As your training supervisor, I just oversee your induction and will not be around after this initial week.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Some of you may be interested to know that the library is offering specialized training sessions on writing a dissertation.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Obviously, this is not relevant to those of you who are undergraduates.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It is just for postgraduates.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Your department will discuss the planning stage of the dissertation, i.e. what you're going to do with you, and we will focus on the structure of it.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: However, the training will also include some time on the computers.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: I realize most of you know how to organize files, but we can show you the different ways to run data programs.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Your tutors will tell you at the outset how to set out the chapters they require, but you'll need to ask them how they would like you to organize the bibliography, because it varies depending on your subject area.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: When you've got something together, the trainer here will look through the draft version for you to see if it's OK.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And one final point, for those of you who have registered from abroad, we can offer individual sessions on dissertations if you feel you need them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: If you require language lessons, then they are available from the International Center next to the Law Department.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: During the first week of term, students are invited to
options: be shown round the library by the librarian.
listen to descriptions of library resources.
do an intensive course in the computer centre.

items.title: The speaker warns the students that
options: internet materials can be unreliable.
downloaded information must be acknowledged.
computer access may be limited at times.

items.title: The library is acquiring more CDs as a resource because
options: they are a cheap source of information.
they take up very little space.
they are more up to date than the reference books.

items.title: Students are encouraged to use journals online because
options: the articles do not need to be returned to the shelves.
reading online is cheaper than photocopying articles.
the stock of printed articles is to be reduced.

items.title: Why might some students continue to use reference books?
options: they can be taken away from the library
they provide information unavailable elsewhere
they can be borrowed for an extended loan period

items.title: What is the responsibility of the Training Supervisor?
options: to supervise and support library staff
to provide orientation to the library facilities
to identify needs and inform section managers

text: Which section of the university will help postgraduate students with their dissertations in the following ways?

list: the postgraduate's own department or tutor
